title: notifications-in-app — Eloquent observers
description: Observers hooking the shared `notifications::NotificationDelivery` table. This module does not own its own model — the base `notifications` module owns NotificationDelivery, and this transport reacts to lifecycle changes on rows where `channel='in_app'`.

hooks.created: When a NotificationDelivery with channel=in_app is created, queue the WebSocket broadcast for its recipient + increment the per-user unread-count cache.
hooks.updated: When read_at transitions from null → non-null: fire InAppDeliveryRead + decrement the unread-count cache. When dismissed_at transitions: fire InAppDeliveryDismissed.

notes: Only ONE observer instance runs per row change (Laravel's built-in event dispatch). The guard `channel = 'in_app'` is enforced inside the observer body (early return on non-in-app channels) so the base notifications module still owns unified state transitions.
The observer NEVER writes back to the model — all writes go through service methods to keep audit-trail attribution consistent.
